name: TMDB_Entertainment_Discovery
description: Discovers, searches, and retrieves comprehensive movie and TV show information from TMDB as the Mind's entertainment content layer. Enables trending analysis, keyword-based media search, detailed metadata retrieval including cast and crew, and multi-regional entertainment exploration for media research and discovery workflows.
progressive_unlock: Basic movie and TV search available immediately. Trending analysis and popular content discovery unlock after Mind has performed at least one successful search. Detailed metadata retrieval (cast, crew, videos) unlocks after trending content analysis. Person trending data unlocks after comprehensive media discovery patterns established.
constraints: Never expose TMDB API key in logs or outputs
Respect TMDB rate limit of approximately 40 requests per 10 seconds (4 req/sec average)
Default to English language ('en') unless explicit language preference specified by Steward
Limit search results to 20 maximum per request to optimize API quota usage
Cache trending content for 6 hours to reduce redundant API calls and respect rate limits
Ensure movie/TV details include credits, videos, and images for comprehensive metadata retrieval
Store popular search queries in STREAM.entity for trend analysis and Steward preferences
Image URLs must use TMDB image base URL (https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/{size}/) with appropriate size parameter
Do not return restricted content without explicit Steward approval in TENET
Always include release dates, ratings, and synopsis in search result summaries

steps.instruction: Validate input against Layer 3 Tool Schema. Confirm required parameters present and correctly typed.
Retrieve TMDB API key from TENET.apiKey.tmdb. Prepare Authorization header as 'Bearer {key}' for API requests.

Determine intent: search_movie (movie title search), search_tv (TV show search), trending (popular content analysis), popular_movies (popularity-based discovery), movie_details (comprehensive movie metadata), or tv_details (comprehensive TV metadata).

For movie search: execute TMDB_SearchMovies with keyword, apply language filter (default 'en' if not specified). Apply optional year and region filters. Limit to page 1-5 for performance. Log search query in STREAM.searches for trend analysis.

For TV search: execute TMDB_SearchTV with keyword and language filter. Apply optional first_air_date_year filter. Limit pagination to first 5 pages. Store search patterns in STREAM for viewing preference analysis.

For trending: execute TMDB_GetTrending with media_type (default 'all') and time_window (default 'day'). Check STREAM.cache for cached results (6-hour TTL). If cache miss, retrieve and store results with timestamp. Return top 20 results with popularity scores.

For popular movies: execute TMDB_GetPopular with language and optional region filters. This endpoint provides popularity-ranked movies ideal for discovery workflows. Cache results with 6-hour TTL. Can be called with pagination for extended browsing.

For movie details: execute TMDB_GetMovieDetails with movie_id and append_to_response=['credits,videos,images']. This retrieves comprehensive metadata including cast, crew, trailers, posters, backdrops, and all supplementary information.

For TV details: execute TMDB_GetTVDetails with series_id and append_to_response=['credits,videos,images']. This retrieves full series information including episode data, cast, crew, videos, and visual assets.

Parse API response and extract media metadata: id, title, release_date, overview, vote_average, poster_path, backdrop_path, genres. For details responses, also extract: cast (top 10), crew (director/writers), videos (trailers), images (posters/backdrops).

Construct image URLs using TMDB image base URL format: https://image.tmdb.org/t/p/{size}/path. Use sizes: w154 (small), w342 (medium), w500 (large), original (full resolution). Default to w342 for search results, w500 for detail views.

Compile entertainment discovery summary with search query (if applicable), result count, trending scores, release year distribution, and genre breakdown. Structure results with title, release_date, rating (vote_average), synopsis, image_url, media_type (movie/tv), and source='TMDB'. Push media usage logs to LTM with key: 'tmdb_discovery_{date}'. Update STREAM with popular movie/TV IDs for quick reference.
